description:
A dataset researcher has three datasets already placed in the directory
    /home/user/datasets

    Current structure:
      /home/user/datasets/astronomy/galaxies.tsv
      /home/user/datasets/climate/temp.csv
      /home/user/datasets/climate/rainfall.csv
      /home/user/datasets/epidemiology/flu_2020.csv
      /home/user/datasets/epidemiology/covid_2020.csv
      /home/user/datasets/epidemiology/measles_2019.csv

    The researcher wants a quick manifest that, for every FIRST-LEVEL sub-directory 
    inside /home/user/datasets, shows the directory name and the number of regular 
    files (not sub-directories) that live directly inside it.

    Requirements for the manifest file:
    1. Location: /home/user/dataset_manifest.tsv
    2. Format: one line per dataset, exactly two fields per line, separated by a single TAB
       character (ASCII 0x09, not spaces).  
          <dataset_directory_name><TAB><file_count>\n  
       Example of ONE correctly formatted line (do not put a space around the tab):
          climate<TAB>2
    3. Lines must be sorted alphabetically by the dataset directory name.
    4. The file must end with a single newline character.
    5. No extra blank lines or leading/trailing spaces anywhere.
    
    Produce that file with a single (possibly pipelined) shell command.  
    After running the command, verify that /home/user/dataset_manifest.tsv exactly
    matches the format described above.

    Once the file exists, your job is complete.
truth:
Initial state prior to agent start:
      Directories and files are present exactly as listed in the task description.
      All files are regular files and readable by the user.
    
    Expected final state for automated grading:
      A new regular file   /home/user/dataset_manifest.tsv   exists with
      read/write permissions for the user and containing the following three
      lines (each terminated with a single LF “\n” and nothing else):

astronomy<TAB>1\n
climate<TAB>2\n
epidemiology<TAB>3\n

      Where <TAB> represents a single ASCII 0x09 character.
